title: CENTCOM Reports 49 Ships Redirected Under Iran Port Blockade as Enforcement Intensifies
summary: US Central Command announced that 49 commercial vessels had been redirected under its blockade of Iranian ports, demonstrating sustained enforcement capability. The blockade, imposed April 13, continues to choke Iranian oil exports and revenue.
contents:
## US Naval Blockade Enforcement Update

### CENTCOM Statement
US Central Command issued an operational update on May 3, 2026, reporting cumulative enforcement results of the naval blockade on Iranian ports.

### Enforcement Statistics
- **Vessels redirected:** 49 commercial ships told to turn back
- **Blockade start date:** April 13, 2026
- **Duration:** 20 days of continuous enforcement
- **CENTCOM statement:** "US forces remain fully committed to total enforcement"

### Economic Impact on Iran
US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent provided economic impact assessment:
- Iran collected "less than $1.3 million in tolls" from strait passage fees
- Iranian oil storage approaching capacity
- Well shutdowns anticipated "within the next week"
- Revenue represents a fraction of pre-war daily oil income

### Maritime Environment
The blockade operates alongside Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, creating a dual constraint on maritime traffic. Windward maritime intelligence data shows:
- Gulf vessel presence at 902 ships (as of April 29)
- Dark vessel activity surging to 170 events
- Transit through Hormuz increasing but remaining "uneven and enforcement-driven"
- Iranian deceptive shipping practices intensifying, including AIS spoofing

### Force Posture
The enforcement operation involves guided-missile destroyers, over 100 aircraft, and 15,000 service members, complementing the newly announced "Project Freedom" escort initiative.
